regions: the northern Ganges floodplain kala-azar belt
golden_hour: Not acute, but PKDL patients are a hidden reservoir that keeps kala-azar circulating. Early diagnosis and full treatment protect the whole community.
summary: A skin condition appearing months to years after treated kala-azar (visceral leishmaniasis), with pale patches and nodules that carry the parasite. Bangladesh has driven kala-azar down but PKDL cases sustain transmission via sandflies. Endemic in the northern floodplain.
symptoms: Pale (hypopigmented) flat patches, often on the face
Raised nodules and papules on face and body
History of kala-azar in the past
causes: Leishmania donovani parasite persisting in skin after visceral kala-azar, transmitted by sandfly bites
risk_factors: Living in an endemic northern district
red_flags: New pale patches or nodules in someone with past kala-azar
treatment_overview: Diagnosis by clinical picture plus history of kala-azar and skin slit-smear/PCR. Treatment with miltefosine or liposomal amphotericin B under the national program, provided free. Completing the full course is essential to break transmission.
prevention: Complete kala-azar treatment fully
Sandfly control with sprayed and insecticide-treated nets
Early detection and treatment of PKDL cases
Improve mud-house living conditions
bd_context: Bangladesh reached kala-azar elimination targets, but PKDL surveillance in Mymensingh and neighbouring districts is key to staying there. The national leishmaniasis program provides free diagnosis and treatment.

qa.q: Kalajor er por chamray dag keno hoy?
qa.a: Kalajor sere gele o kichu Leishmania parasite chamrায় theke jete pare, ja pore fyakashe dag ba chaka hisebe dekha dey - eke PKDL bola hoy. Eta betha ba চুলকানি chara i thake. Purbe kalajor hoye thakle notun chamra dag hole doctor dekhান।

qa.q: Is PKDL contagious to others?
qa.a: Not by touch, but PKDL skin carries the parasite, and a sandfly biting the patient can pass it to others. That is why treating PKDL fully is important to stop kala-azar returning in the community.

qa.q: PKDL er cikitsha ki free?
qa.a: Ha, national leishmaniasis program er madhome PKDL er diagnosis o cikitsha (miltefosine ba amphotericin B) free deওয়া hoy. Puro course sesh kora khub joruri jate parasite sompurno noshto hoy.

qa.q: Which areas have kala-azar and PKDL?
qa.a: The northern Ganges floodplain - Mymensingh, Tangail, Pabna, and Sirajganj - is the historic kala-azar belt. PKDL surveillance there prevents new outbreaks. Sandfly control in mud-house areas is central.

qa.q: PKDL er dag ki nijei sere jay?
qa.a: Sadharonoto nijei puropuri sere jay na ebong cikitsa chara parasite theke jay. Tai obohela na kore national program er cikitsha neওয়া uchit - eta bepokta o valo hoy free te.

qa.q: How is PKDL diagnosed?
qa.a: By the clinical appearance of pale patches or nodules, a history of past kala-azar, and lab confirmation via skin slit-smear or PCR. A dermatologist or the leishmaniasis program can arrange this.

qa.q: Sandfly theke ki kore bachbo?
qa.a: Insecticide-treated moshari bebohar korun, ghor er bhitor spray korান, ebong mati/kada ghor er obostha unnoto korun. Sandfly beshi hoy godhuli o ratre, tai ei somoy sotorko thakun.

qa.q: Can PKDL come back after treatment?
qa.a: Relapse is uncommon if the full course is completed, but incomplete treatment risks recurrence and continued transmission. Follow-up after treatment is part of the program.

qa.q: PKDL ki proanघातক?
qa.a: PKDL nijei sadharonoto jibonহানিকর noy - visceral kala-azar er moto noy. Kintu eta community te kala-azar chalu rakhe, ebong mukher dag/chaka cosmetic o samajik somossa toiri kore.

qa.q: Where do I report a suspected case?
qa.a: At the nearest Upazila Health Complex or district hospital, which link to the national leishmaniasis elimination program. Reporting suspected PKDL helps stop community spread.

regions: higher in diabetes and hypertension hotspots
golden_hour: Not acute, but the golden action is early detection. Catching CKD at stage 1-3 by checking creatinine and urine protein can slow or stop progression to dialysis.
summary: Progressive, permanent loss of kidney function, driven in Bangladesh mainly by diabetes and high blood pressure, plus overuse of painkillers and unregulated herbal/kobiraji medicines. Often silent until advanced. Dialysis and transplant are scarce and costly, so prevention and early control are vital.
symptoms: Swelling of legs, face, or around eyes
risk_factors: Long-term NSAID or unregulated medicine use
complications: Kidney failure requiring dialysis or transplant
red_flags: Chest pain with high potassium risk
treatment_overview: Control the underlying cause: tight diabetes and blood pressure control (often with ACE inhibitors/ARBs), avoid NSAIDs and unregulated remedies, low-salt diet, and treat anemia and bone disease. Advanced disease needs dialysis or transplant. SGLT2 inhibitors now slow progression in many patients.
prevention: Control diabetes and blood pressure
Avoid regular painkillers and unproven kobiraji/herbal medicines
Stay hydrated, limit salt
Annual creatinine and urine test if at risk
Treat infections and stones promptly
bd_context: Kidney units at BSMMU, NIKDU, and Kidney Foundation Bangladesh provide dialysis, but capacity is limited and costs are high. Many patients present too late. Screening at-risk people (diabetics, hypertensives) is the most impactful step.

qa.q: Pa o mukh fule jacche, kidney somossa hote pare?
qa.a: Ha, pa, mukh ba chokher niche fola kidney roger ekta lokkhon hote pare, bishesh kore prosrab kome jaওয়া ba fenা fenা prosrab er sathe. Diabetes ba presar thakle creatinine o urine test koriye nin - age dhora porle kidney bachানো jay.

qa.q: Does taking painkillers damage kidneys?
qa.a: Yes. Regular use of NSAID painkillers (like diclofenac, ibuprofen) can harm the kidneys, especially with diabetes, hypertension, or dehydration. Avoid routine painkillers and never take long courses without a doctor - use paracetamol for simple pain when needed.

qa.q: Kidney valo rakhte ki korbo?
qa.a: Diabetes o presar niyontrone rakhun, lobon kom khan, painkiller o oproমাণিত herbal/kobiraji oshudh eড়িয়ে chola, pani thik moto khান, ebong bochore ekbar creatinine o urine test korান - bishesh kore ঝুঁkipurno hole.

qa.q: Is kidney disease reversible?
qa.a: Early-stage kidney disease can often be stabilized or slowed if the cause (diabetes, blood pressure) is controlled. But advanced CKD is permanent and may need dialysis or transplant - which is why early detection matters so much.

qa.q: Kobiraji oshudh e ki kidney kharap hoy?
qa.a: Ha, onek oproমাণিত herbal ba kobiraji oshudhe bhari dhatu ba kidney-khoti kora upadan thake. Nirapod na jene erokom oshudh dirghodin khele kidney bikol hote pare. Doctor er poramorsho chara erokom oshudh eড়িয়ে chalun.

qa.q: How often should a diabetic check kidneys?
qa.a: At least once a year, with a blood creatinine (eGFR) and a urine test for protein (albumin). Earlier detection lets treatment slow the damage before it reaches dialysis stage.

qa.q: Dialysis chara ki bhalo thaka jay?
qa.a: Suru o modhyo porjaye onek rogi thik moto oshudh, khabar niyontron o cause control kore dialysis chara bochorer por bochor valo thake. Sudhu obohela korle i eta druto dialysis porjaye chole jay.

qa.q: What diet is good for kidney patients?
qa.a: Generally low salt, controlled protein, and limited potassium/phosphate in advanced stages (avoid excess banana, coconut water, dairy if advised). A nephrologist or dietitian should tailor this to your stage - do not self-restrict severely.

qa.q: Kidney fail hole ki hoy?
qa.a: Kidney bikol hole shorire boju o pani jome, potassium bere hridpinder ঝুঁki toiri hoy, o bish jome durbolota o ojnানota ashe. Tokhon dialysis ba kidney transplant lage. Tai age theke i control kora joruri.

qa.q: Where can I get dialysis in Bangladesh?
qa.a: At NIKDU, BSMMU, Kidney Foundation Bangladesh, and dialysis units in major hospitals. Capacity is limited and cost is significant, so preventing progression through early control is strongly preferred.

golden_hour: Not acute. Early iodine correction prevents goiter and protects children's brain development - the golden action is using iodized salt lifelong, especially in pregnancy.
summary: Enlargement of the thyroid gland (neck swelling), classically from dietary iodine deficiency in areas with iodine-poor soil and water. Bangladesh's universal salt iodization reduced it greatly, but pockets remain. Iodine deficiency in pregnancy harms fetal brain development.
symptoms: Visible swelling at the front of the neck
Tightness or pressure in the neck
Difficulty swallowing or breathing if large
Sometimes symptoms of underactive thyroid (tiredness, weight gain, cold intolerance)
Hoarse voice if pressing on nerves
complications: In pregnancy: miscarriage, stillbirth, and impaired child brain development (cretinism)
red_flags: Hoarse voice or a lump with weight loss
treatment_overview: Correct iodine intake with iodized salt; iodine or thyroid hormone replacement if deficient/hypothyroid. Large or compressive goiters, or suspicious nodules, need thyroid function tests, ultrasound, and sometimes surgery. Pregnant women need adequate iodine.
prevention: Use iodized salt in every household
Adequate iodine in pregnancy (as advised)
Eat sea fish and iodine-rich foods where possible
Community salt iodization monitoring
bd_context: Universal Salt Iodization has been a major public-health success in Bangladesh, but iodine-poor hill and haor areas and low-quality non-iodized salt still cause cases. Endocrine clinics and thyroid ultrasound are available in district and city hospitals.

qa.q: Gola fule geche - eta ki thyroid er somossa?
qa.a: Golar samner dike fola sadharonoto thyroid gronti boro howa (goiter) - onek somoy iodine er obhabe hoy. Thyroid test o ultrasound kore karon jana jay. Druto barche ba shokto/fixed chaka hole obosshoi doctor dekhান।

qa.q: Does iodized salt prevent goiter?
qa.a: Yes. Using properly iodized salt daily is the main prevention for iodine-deficiency goiter, and it protects children's brain development. Buy branded iodized salt and store it dry and covered so the iodine is not lost.

qa.q: Gorbhoboti obosthায় iodine keno joruri?
qa.a: Gorbhabosthায় iodine er obhabe bacchar mostisker gothon khoti hote pare (cretinism), miscarriage ba mrito bacchar ঝুঁki bare. Tai gorbhoboti der iodized salt ebong doctor er poramorsho moto iodine neওয়া joruri.

qa.q: Is every neck swelling a thyroid problem?
qa.a: No - neck swelling can be from lymph nodes, cysts, or infections too. But a swelling at the front-centre of the neck that moves when you swallow is usually thyroid. A doctor and an ultrasound can tell the difference.

qa.q: Goiter er cikitsha ki operation lage?
qa.a: Sob goiter e operation lage na. Chhoto o iodine-obhab jonito hole iodine/thyroid oshudhe thik hote pare. Kintu boro, sash/dhok e chap deওয়া, ba sondehojonok nodule hole operation lagte pare.

qa.q: Which areas of Bangladesh have iodine deficiency?
qa.a: Iodine-poor soil areas, particularly northern hill and haor regions and parts of Sylhet and Mymensingh, historically had more goiter. Salt iodization reduced this, but low-quality non-iodized salt still causes cases.

qa.q: Thyroid boro hole ki hormone somossa hoy?
qa.a: Kokhono kokhono goiter er sathe thyroid kom kaj kore (hypothyroid) - tokhon klanti, ojon bara, thanda laga hoy. Abar kono kono khetre beshi kaj kore. Tai TSH test kore hormone obostha jana joruri.

qa.q: Can goiter cause cancer?
qa.a: Most goiters are benign, but a hard, fixed, or rapidly growing nodule, or one with hoarse voice and weight loss, needs evaluation to rule out thyroid cancer. Ultrasound and sometimes a needle biopsy (FNAC) are used.

qa.q: Iodine besh khele ki khoti?
qa.a: Doorকার er cheye onek beshi iodine (jemon oniyontrito supplement) thyroid er somossa banate pare. Sadharonoto iodized salt e joto iodine thake tai jথেষ্ট - alada supplement doctor er poramorsho chara neben na.

qa.q: Where do I get thyroid tests?
qa.a: Thyroid function tests (TSH, T3, T4) and neck ultrasound are available at district hospitals, medical colleges, and diagnostic centres. An endocrinologist manages larger or hormone-related goiters.

regions: rural areas with stray dogs highest
golden_hour: Rabies is nearly 100% fatal once symptoms start but almost 100% preventable if you act fast. The golden action is immediate wound washing with soap and running water for 15 minutes, then starting post-exposure vaccine the same day.
summary: A fatal viral brain infection transmitted by the bite or scratch of an infected animal, usually a dog. Bangladesh has reduced deaths through free vaccine and dog vaccination, but rural bites still kill. Once symptoms appear there is no cure, so prevention after a bite is everything.
symptoms: (After a bite) initially none for weeks
Tingling or pain at the bite site
causes: Rabies virus from the saliva of an infected animal via bite, scratch, or lick on broken skin - mainly dogs, also cats, jackals, bats, mongoose
risk_factors: Dog or animal bite, especially unprovoked
Delay in wound care and vaccination
complications: There is no effective treatment after symptom onset
red_flags: Any bite/scratch from a stray or unknown animal
Deep bite, bite on face/hands, or bleeding wound
Behavioural change in the biting animal
treatment_overview: Immediately wash the wound with soap and running water for 15 minutes. Do NOT close the wound tightly. Go the same day for post-exposure prophylaxis (PEP): a course of rabies vaccine, plus rabies immunoglobulin for severe/category III bites. This is provided FREE at government hospitals. Full course must be completed.
prevention: Vaccinate pet dogs and cats
Avoid stray animals
Teach children not to provoke dogs
Immediate wound care and vaccination after any bite
Report and manage stray dog populations
bd_context: Bangladesh's national rabies control program provides FREE vaccine and immunoglobulin at medical college hospitals, Sadar hospitals, and the Infectious Disease Hospital (Mohakhali). Mass dog vaccination has cut deaths, but rural late presentation remains deadly.

qa.q: Kukure kamor dile prothome ki korbo?
qa.a: Sathe sathe khôt sabun o cholman pani diye 15 minute dhore dhue felun - eta virus onek komay. Khôt shokto kore bedhe felben na. Oi din i hospital e giye rabies vaccine shuru korun. Deri korben na - jolatonko shuru hole ar cure nei.

qa.q: Is the rabies vaccine free in Bangladesh?
qa.a: Yes. Post-exposure rabies vaccine, and immunoglobulin for severe bites, are provided free at government medical college hospitals, Sadar hospitals, and the Infectious Disease Hospital in Mohakhali. Go the same day as the bite.

qa.q: Kukur ta sustho dekhale ki vaccine lagbe?
qa.a: Ha, tobuo vaccine shuru korা nirapod, karon kukur pore osusto hote pare ba dekhe bujha nao jete pare. Kukur ke 10 din observe kora jete pare, kintu vaccine deri korben na - doctor er poramorsho nin.

qa.q: How many rabies injections are needed?
qa.a: A full post-exposure course is several doses over about a month (commonly on days 0, 3, 7, and 14-28, depending on the schedule). Severe bites also need immunoglobulin at the wound. Complete the whole course - stopping early leaves you at risk.

qa.q: Biral er achor ba lehon e ki rabies hote pare?
qa.a: Ha, biral ba onno prani r achor, kamor, ba khôt-e lehon theke o rabies hote pare. Bhanga chamrায় lehon o ঝুঁkipurno. Ei rokom hole o khôt dhue vaccine niye nin.

qa.q: Can rabies be cured after symptoms start?
qa.a: No. Once rabies symptoms (fear of water, agitation, paralysis) appear, it is almost always fatal. That is why immediate wound washing and same-day vaccination after a bite are absolutely critical - prevention is the only real protection.

qa.q: Khôt e ki morich ba onno kichu debo?
qa.a: Na, khôt e morich, chun, mati ba oijopadi kichu deben na - eta khoti kore o infection barায়. Sudhu sabun o cholman pani diye 15 minute dhue, tarpor hospital e jan.

qa.q: How long after a bite can I still take the vaccine?
qa.a: Start as soon as possible - ideally the same day. But even if some days have passed and no symptoms have appeared, you should still start the vaccine course immediately. It is never too late until symptoms begin.

qa.q: Bacchader kamor dile ki alada kichu?
qa.a: Bacchara beshi kamor khay ebong onek somoy bola bhule jay ba lukiye rakhe. Kamor mukh, matha ba hate hole beshi bipojjonok. Baccha ke kukur/biral kamranোle obosshoi doctor ke bolun o vaccine korান।

qa.q: Where do I get rabies treatment in Dhaka?
qa.a: The Infectious Disease Hospital in Mohakhali is the main centre, and medical college and Sadar hospitals also provide free rabies PEP. Go immediately after washing the wound.

golden_hour: Not minute-critical, but early correct diagnosis and appropriate antibiotics prevent dangerous complications like intestinal perforation around week 3.
summary: A bacterial infection from contaminated food and water caused by Salmonella Typhi, extremely common in Bangladesh. A major concern now is rising antibiotic resistance, including XDR strains, making self-medication with random antibiotics dangerous. Vaccination and clean water are key.
symptoms: Relative slow pulse for the fever
causes: Salmonella Typhi/Paratyphi from food or water contaminated with human feces
risk_factors: Unsafe drinking water and street food
red_flags: Severe abdominal pain and distension (possible perforation)
Persistent high fever not responding to treatment
treatment_overview: Diagnosis with blood culture (best) rather than Widal alone. Treatment with an appropriate antibiotic guided by local resistance - do NOT self-medicate, as wrong or partial antibiotics fuel resistance. Adequate hydration, nutrition, and monitoring for complications. Hospital care for severe or XDR cases.
prevention: Drink safe/boiled/filtered water
Avoid unsafe street food and cut fruit
Typhoid conjugate vaccine (TCV)
Handwashing and good sanitation
Treat carriers
bd_context: Typhoid is endemic across Bangladesh, especially in dense urban areas. Antibiotic resistance is rising, so blood culture-guided treatment matters. The typhoid conjugate vaccine has been introduced in the national program for children.

qa.q: Typhoid e nijei antibiotic khaওয়া ki thik?
qa.a: Na. Nijei random antibiotic khele bhul oshudh ba adha course e resistance bere jay, ja Bangladesh e boro somossa (XDR typhoid). Doctor dekhiye, sombhob hole blood culture kore, thik antibiotic puro course khান।

qa.q: How is typhoid best diagnosed?
qa.a: Blood culture is the most reliable test, especially in the first week. The Widal test alone is often misleading in an endemic country like Bangladesh. A doctor interprets tests together with your symptoms.

qa.q: Typhoid koto din e valo hoy?
qa.a: Thik antibiotic e sadharonoto 1-2 soptahe jor komte shuru kore, kintu durbolota kichudin thakte pare. Puro course sesh na korle relapse ba complication hote pare. Tesorosho soptahe pet er jotil somossa (perforation) er dike khyal rakhun.

qa.q: Is there a vaccine for typhoid?
qa.a: Yes, the typhoid conjugate vaccine (TCV) protects against typhoid and has been introduced for children in Bangladesh. Vaccination plus safe water and food is the best prevention, especially in high-risk areas.

qa.q: XDR typhoid mane ki?
qa.a: XDR mane extensively drug-resistant - er mane sadharon typhoid antibiotic gulo ei strain er upor kaj kore na. Eta cikitsha kothin o dami kore. Tai self-medication eড়িয়ে chalun ebong doctor er guideline mane antibiotic khান।

qa.q: What food should I eat during typhoid?
qa.a: Soft, easy-to-digest, hygienic food - khichuri, rice, banana, soups - with plenty of safe fluids. Avoid heavy, oily, and raw street food. Good nutrition supports recovery, and hydration is important with fever.

qa.q: Typhoid ki bar bar hote pare?
qa.a: Ha, purno cikitsha na hole relapse hote pare, ebong notun kore o abar sonkromon hote pare jodi dushito pani/khabar khan. Kichu manush 'carrier' hoye onno der e chorate pare, tader alada cikitsha lage.

qa.q: How do I prevent typhoid at home?
qa.a: Drink boiled or properly filtered water, avoid unsafe street food and cut fruit, wash hands before eating and after the toilet, and vaccinate children. Safe water and sanitation cut typhoid dramatically.

qa.q: Jor er sathe pet betha o koshtho kathinno - typhoid?
qa.a: Dirghomeyadi (soptaho dhore) baড়তে thaka jor, matha betha, durbolota, pet betha o kostho kathinno ba diarrhea typhoid er lokkhon hote pare. Nishchit hote blood test o doctor er poramorsho nin - nijei antibiotic khaben na.

qa.q: When does typhoid become an emergency?
qa.a: If there is severe abdominal pain and distension (possible intestinal perforation), blood in stool, confusion, or persistent high fever despite treatment - go to hospital urgently. These usually occur if typhoid is untreated or mistreated.

name_en: Chronic Bronchitis / COPD from Air Pollution and Biomass Smoke
name_bn: বায়ু দূষণ ও চুলার ধোঁয়া জনিত দীর্ঘমেয়াদি ফুসফুস রোগ
golden_hour: Not acute, but stopping the exposure (smoking, indoor smoke, polluted air) early prevents irreversible lung damage. Acute severe breathlessness is an emergency needing hospital care.
summary: Long-term lung damage and airway narrowing from smoking, household biomass (wood/dung/straw) cooking smoke, and Bangladesh's severe outdoor air pollution. A leading cause of chronic breathlessness and death, affecting even non-smoking women who cook over traditional stoves.
symptoms: Chronic cough with phlegm, especially mornings
causes: Outdoor air pollution (PM2.5, brick-kiln and traffic emissions)
risk_factors: Cooking over wood, dung, or straw indoors (especially women)
Working near brick kilns, dust, or fumes
treatment_overview: Stop smoking and reduce smoke/pollution exposure (the single most important step). Inhalers (bronchodilators, sometimes steroids), pulmonary rehab, vaccination against flu/pneumonia, and prompt treatment of flare-ups. Oxygen for advanced disease. Acute severe attacks need emergency care.
prevention: Do not smoke; avoid secondhand smoke
Use improved/clean cookstoves or LPG instead of biomass
Ventilate kitchens; cook outside where possible
Wear a mask on high-pollution days
Treat chest infections early
bd_context: Dhaka regularly ranks among the world's most polluted cities, and millions of rural women cook over smoky stoves, so COPD/chronic bronchitis is very common - including in non-smokers. Chest medicine departments and the National Institute of Diseases of the Chest and Hospital (NIDCH) manage it.

qa.q: Chulার dhoya theke ki fusfusের khoti hoy?
qa.a: Ha, kath, khori ba goitার lakri diye ranna korle je dhoya hoy ta dirghodine fusfus khoti kore - tai onek meye ja dhumpan koren na tader o COPD/chronic bronchitis hoy. Unnoto chula ba gas bebohar korun ebong rannaghor bataশযুক্ত rakhun.

qa.q: How do I know if my cough is COPD?
qa.a: A cough with phlegm lasting most days for months, with breathlessness that worsens over years, especially in a smoker or someone exposed to cooking/industrial smoke, suggests chronic bronchitis/COPD. A doctor confirms it with a lung function test (spirometry).

qa.q: Dhumpan chere dile ki fusfus valo hobe?
qa.a: Je khoti hoye geche ta puropuri thik nao hote pare, kintu dhumpan chere dile khoti aro barা bondho hoy o obostha onek valo thake. Ja bয়সেই chaড়ুন na keno, upokar hoy - eta sobcheye guruttopurno step.

qa.q: Is Dhaka air pollution really harming my lungs?
qa.a: Yes. Dhaka's high PM2.5 pollution damages lungs and worsens asthma, bronchitis, and COPD. On high-pollution days, wear a good mask, limit outdoor exertion, and keep windows closed - and treat any breathing worsening promptly.

qa.q: COPD te inhaler keno lage?
qa.a: Inhaler oshudh soja fusfuse pouছে airway khule dey o sash neওয়া sohoj kore, kom side-effect e. Thik niyome inhaler bebohar korle haf dhora komay o flare-up kome. Doctor dekhiye thik technique shikhe nin.

qa.q: When is breathlessness an emergency?
qa.a: If you have severe breathlessness at rest, blue lips or fingers, confusion or drowsiness, or you are coughing blood - go to hospital immediately. A sudden worsening (exacerbation), often with fever, also needs urgent care.

qa.q: Unnoto chula ki really upokari?
qa.a: Ha, improved cookstove ba LPG gas rannaghore dhoya onek komay, ফলে meyeder o bacchader fusfus, chokh o shash er somossa kome. Eta COPD, chokh jala o bacchader pneumonia thekate shohay kore.

qa.q: Can COPD patients get vaccines?
qa.a: Yes, and they should. Annual flu vaccine and pneumococcal (pneumonia) vaccine reduce dangerous chest infections and flare-ups in COPD and chronic bronchitis. Ask your doctor about these.

qa.q: Kashi te rokto ashle ki korbo?
qa.a: Kashi te rokto ashা kokhonoi obohela korবেন na - eta TB, fusfus infection ba onno gurutar somossar lokkhon hote pare. Sathe sathe doctor dekhিয়ে buker X-ray o test korান।

qa.q: Where is COPD treated in Bangladesh?
qa.a: Chest medicine (respiratory) departments in medical college hospitals and the National Institute of Diseases of the Chest and Hospital (NIDCH) in Dhaka manage COPD, along with local physicians for routine inhaler care.
